_doc: Each entry registers ONE deterministic computation: a metric formula,
a Postgres RPC, a Python pure function. The contract makes the math
auditable: any downstream dashboard or AI agent can cite the formula_id
and the standards reference (ISO 14224, SAE JA1011, etc.), and
regression tests can pin the math.
name            human-readable name ('MTBF — Mean Time Between Failures')
inputs          list of capture_ids / column refs the formula reads
implemented_in  RPC name OR Python function OR migration filename
Auditor: tools/audit_formula_contracts.py — surfaces RPCs that look
like deterministic metrics but aren't yet registered.
Seed entries below are the formulas already documented in KPI_ENGINE.md.

formulas.name: MTBF — Mean Time Between Failures
formulas.partial_reason: Uses CALENDAR time intervals (logbook.created_at) rather than OPERATING time per ISO 14224 §9.3. Operating-time mode requires per-asset uptime telemetry not yet captured.
formulas.implemented_in: RPC: get_mtbf_by_machine + view: v_kpi_truth (partial — calendar time, not operating time)
formulas.description: Mean of inter-arrival intervals of corrective entries within a window. Same algorithm shared by the interactive RPC (user-selected window) and the materialised view (snapshot at 30/90/365 days).

formulas.name: MTTR — Mean Time To Repair
formulas.partial_reason: Uses logbook.downtime_hours which is TOTAL downtime (active repair + logistic + admin). ISO 14224 §9.4 specifies ACTIVE REPAIR TIME only. Implementation is therefore the broader MTTR_total variant.
formulas.implemented_in: RPC: get_mttr_by_machine (partial — TOTAL downtime, not active repair time only)
formulas.description: Mean downtime_hours of corrective entries whose status = Closed within the window.

formulas.name: PM Compliance — 30-day floor
formulas.partial_reason: 30-day threshold is a PLATFORM convention, not the SMRP standard. SMRP 2.1.1 is per-asset-per-PM-cycle on the asset's actual frequency. The boolean is_due flag is a coarse approximation used for at-a-glance overdue rendering; period-scoped pct variants (30/90/365) are derived elsewhere.
formulas.implemented_in: view: v_pm_compliance_truth (is_due column) (partial — 30-day floor approximation, not SMRP per-cycle pct)
formulas.description: is_due = (last_anchor_date IS NULL) OR (last_anchor_date < now - 30 days). Used by every PM-overdue tile across the platform; period-scoped variants for 30/90/365 day windows are derived from the same anchor.

formulas.name: Risk Score (composite, 6-factor)
formulas.standard_cite: SAE JA1011 §5.4 + IEC 60812 RPN + Weibull wear-out
formulas.partial_reason: Composite weighting (0.30/0.30/0.20/0.20/0.15/0.10) is a PLATFORM heuristic blending SAE JA1011 consequence-class + IEC 60812 RPN + Weibull β. No single standard prescribes these weights; they were calibrated against the seed dataset. Document explicitly as platform composite, not standard.
formulas.implemented_in: edge fn: batch-risk-scoring (writes asset_risk_scores) + view: v_risk_truth (partial — platform-calibrated composite blend, not a single standard's prescription)
formulas.description: Weighted composite — PM overdue 0.30, fault frequency 0.30, time-to-MTBF 0.20, repeat fault 0.20, Weibull wear-out 0.15, FMEA top RPN 0.10 — clamped to [0, 1]. Daily snapshot.

formulas.name: OEE — Overall Equipment Effectiveness (full)
formulas.implemented_in: RPC: get_oee_by_machine (migration 20260520000002 + 20260520000006) — falls back to partial when performance_pct is NULL
formulas.description: Full OEE = Availability × Performance × Quality. The canonical Nakajima formula. Requires three independent factors; if any factor is null/unavailable the RPC returns the partial form (see oee_iso_22400_partial) and tags the result accordingly.

formulas.name: Bearing L10 Life (hours)
formulas.implemented_in: engineering-design.html bearing-life calculator
formulas.description: L10 = (C/P)^p × 10^6 revolutions, then converted to hours at constant rpm. Ball bearings p=3, roller bearings p=10/3 per ISO 281 §5.

formulas.name: Pipe Pressure Drop (Darcy-Weisbach)
formulas.implemented_in: engineering-design.html pipe pressure drop calculator
formulas.description: Pressure loss in a pipe segment per the Darcy-Weisbach equation. Friction factor from Moody chart / Colebrook-White given Reynolds + roughness.

formulas.name: Pump Total Dynamic Head
formulas.partial_reason: Bernoulli velocity term (v_d² - v_s²)/(2g) is OMITTED in the platform's quick calc — acceptable when discharge and suction pipe diameters are equal, but partial relative to full API 610 §6.1.
formulas.implemented_in: engineering-design.html pump-head calculator (partial — Bernoulli velocity term omitted)
formulas.description: Pump total dynamic head. Platform calc combines pressure + elevation; velocity-head correction is small in equal-diameter systems but should be added for variable-diameter applications.

formulas.name: Three-Phase Motor Output Power
formulas.implemented_in: engineering-design.html motor sizing calculator
formulas.description: Three-phase motor output power per NEMA MG-1. Multiply by 0.746 to convert hp→kW if nameplate is in hp.

formulas.name: Risk Priority Number (FMEA)
formulas.implemented_in: asset-hub.html FMEA panel + rcm_fmea_modes table
formulas.description: Classical FMEA RPN per IEC 60812:2018. The platform also surfaces Action Priority (AIAG-VDA) where the team has registered it.

formulas.name: Sensor Z-Score Anomaly (3-sigma)
formulas.implemented_in: edge fn: sensor-readings-ingest sets quality_flag='ANOMALY' when |z|>3; surfaced via v_sensor_truth.is_anomaly
formulas.description: Sensor reading z-score against a rolling baseline; |z|>3 sets quality_flag='ANOMALY'. The platform's Today's One Thing ranker reads is_anomaly from v_sensor_truth.

formulas.name: Schedule Performance Index (SPI)
formulas.implemented_in: project-manager.html + project-report.html SPI tile
formulas.description: Earned Value / Planned Value. SPI < 1 = behind schedule; SPI > 1 = ahead. PMBOK 7th ed.

formulas.name: Cost Performance Index (CPI)
formulas.implemented_in: project-manager.html + project-report.html CPI tile
formulas.description: Earned Value / Actual Cost. CPI < 1 = over budget; CPI > 1 = under. PMBOK 7th ed.

formulas.name: Adoption Risk Score (composite)
formulas.implemented_in: RPC compute_adoption_risk + v_adoption_truth + hive.html adoption-risk-score tile
formulas.description: Composite hive-level adoption risk = weighted avg of three component risks (supervisor decay / stair stall / new-worker silence). Higher = greater churn risk. Phase 3 adoption observability.

formulas.name: Marketplace Seller Quality Score
formulas.implemented_in: update_seller_rating RPC + marketplace.html quality-score-num tile + marketplace-seller-profile.html
formulas.description: Marketplace-domain seller quality composite. Distinct from OEE Quality (production output) — same word, different domain. Blends Stripe Connect verification + buyer ratings + dispute history.

formulas.name: Platform Health Score
formulas.implemented_in: validate.py + platform_health.json + founder-console.html ring-pct-num + platform-health.html health-num
formulas.description: Platform-internal validator-pass composite. Excludes SKIPs. Drives founder-console ring + platform-health ring. Green ≥85 / amber ≥60 / red <60.

formulas.name: Skill Level Tier
formulas.implemented_in: skillmatrix.html (5-level dots + disc-level-badge per discipline)
formulas.description: 5-tier ladder: Foundation (0-39) / Practitioner (40-59) / Expert (60-74) / Master (75-89) / Sage (90-100). Exam-score driven; distinct from achievement-card level (XP driven).

formulas.name: Skill Exam Score
formulas.standard_cite: WorkHive Skill Tier Model — exam grading
formulas.implemented_in: skill_exam_attempts table + skillmatrix.html result-score
formulas.description: Raw exam score. Feeds skill_level_tier bucketing.

formulas.name: Achievement Card Level
formulas.implemented_in: achievements.html ac-card-level + achievement_xp_log table
formulas.description: Per-card XP-to-level scaling. Each achievement_definition row specifies xp_per_level + max_level.

formulas.name: P-F Interval (days)
formulas.partial_reason: Surface displays the P-F interval directly without showing the safety_factor used to derive the PM interval. PM interval = P-F / (2 × safety_factor) per SAE JA1011 §6.
formulas.implemented_in: asset-hub.html pf-pf-days tile + pf_intervals table (partial — direct P-F display, PM interval derivation not surfaced)
formulas.description: Potential-to-functional failure interval. The platform surfaces P-F directly; the PM interval = P-F / (2 × safety_factor) is computed but not always shown alongside.

formulas.name: Hive Stair Composite Score
formulas.implemented_in: RPC compute_hive_readiness (migration 20260513000001) + v_hive_readiness_truth + hive.html stair card
formulas.description: Composite of 5 dimension scores (reliability / operations / safety / people / cost) averaged and clamped 0-100. Maps to stair 0-5 via bucket thresholds.

formulas.name: OEE — partial (Availability × Quality)
formulas.partial_reason: Performance factor excluded: requires per-asset ideal_cycle_time_seconds (Tier F capture asset_ideal_cycle_time) which is only just shipped. Until adoption reaches a baseline, the RPC returns partial OEE = A × Q and tags is_partial=true so dashboards label honestly.
formulas.implemented_in: python-api: descriptive.calc_oee + RPC get_oee_by_machine (partial branch)
formulas.description: Partial OEE = Availability × Quality. Used when per-asset ideal cycle time (the Performance factor input) is not yet captured. Always declared 'partial' in the source chip so users don't conflate with full OEE. Closing this gap requires capture contract asset_ideal_cycle_time (Tier F next-build).
formulas.added_in: formal contract 2026-05-20 (captures existing platform reality + flags the gap)
